methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
RISE Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing startup founded by Arron Acosta and Blake Sessions that has raised $22M+ to date. The valuation universe includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers in robotics and manufacturing, though the immediate peer set remains limited for direct comparison at this stage.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($22M) as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to subsequent stage rounds yields an implied valuation of $44M. The peer-median method could not be reliably applied; while seven same-sector peers exist in our dataset, none satisfy minimum thresholds for narrow-filter comparability (fewer than three same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier datapoints available).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $37M to $51M, with $44M as the median point. This range reflects the methodological reliance on a single triangulation input and the absence of sufficiently granular peer comparables at RISE's specific stage and tier. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to robotics-sector benchmarks, competitive positioning within the fragmented automation landscape, and path-to-revenue dynamics typical of frontier manufacturing tech. A more refined valuation would benefit from additional private-market transaction data or clearer financial metrics (burn rate, ARR trajectory, customer concentration).
